subject: Add fluent setters for BuildingBlocks and ExecuteBuildingBlock
commit_message:
Add fluent setters for BuildingBlocks and ExecuteBuildingBlock

Test which check more than one functionallity were separarted.
Minor code improvements around BB and EBB.

Issue-ID: SO-2582
Change-Id: I318f8ac299f7204fdd6799108182a29e519724d9
Signed-off-by: kjaniak <kornel.janiak@nokia.com>

(3 comments)

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-2

Why can't fluent setters accomplish this same task on the beans?

e.g.

myObj.withProp1("myValue").withProp2("myValue").withProp3("myValue");

Patch Set 9:

I think the intention was to return an immutable object.

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

> Why can't fluent setters accomplish this same task on the beans?
 > 
 > e.g.
 > 
 > myObj.withProp1("myValue").withProp2("myValue").withProp3("myValue");

The main idea was to introduce builder (less boiler plate code) and Immutable class (Thread safety, faster deserialization time in Jackson). Mutable objects, like Java Beans (i assume fluent setter is something similar) is no longer good coding practice. I base my knowledge for instance on this article: https://octoperf.com/blog/2016/04/07/why-objects-must-be-immutable/ , also this video is good: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FQERMVABRrQ

(3 comments)

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > > Why can't fluent setters accomplish this same task on the beans?
 > >
 > > e.g.
 > >
 > > myObj.withProp1("myValue").withProp2("myValue").withProp3("myValue");
 > 
 > The main idea was to introduce builder (less boiler plate code) and
 > Immutable class (Thread safety, faster deserialization time in
 > Jackson). Mutable objects, like Java Beans (i assume fluent setter
 > is something similar) is no longer good coding practice. I base my
 > knowledge for instance on this article: https://octoperf.com/blog/2016/04/07/why-objects-must-be-immutable/
 > , also this video is good: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FQERMVABRrQ

Hello Max, I have reconsidered usage Immutable objects. I took approach propose by you to start with fluent setters. Please retake my commit. Kornel
